description: Venom role packs. `init` asks what kind of work you do and assembles the right team over the SAME core four + the full memory tier. A pack = the specialists added on top of `core`. This file is tool-agnostic; per-tool model/tool wiring lives in each adapter's manifest. To add a pack (the community extension point): add an entry under `packs` whose `adds` reference roles in `roles`. Users can also start from a pack and add/remove individual roles (e.g. the optional `marketing`).
coreNote: These four + the full agent-memory/ tier are installed with EVERY pack. boss-1 (orchestrator) and boss-2 (independent auditor) are the only agents that talk to the owner; critics and security are the two read-only gates.

web-app.name: Web / App Development
web-app.description: The full software-builder team — research, two parallel builders, testing, debugging, design, and docs.
web-app.bestFor: Web apps, APIs, CLIs, and general software projects.

data-ml.name: Data / ML
data-ml.description: Pipelines and models with honest evaluation — the generic developers and design are dropped for domain-specific builders.
data-ml.bestFor: Data pipelines, ETL, and model training/evaluation.

research-academic.name: Research / Academic
research-academic.description: An advisory research room — surveys prior work, designs sound methods, and writes; no code builders.
research-academic.bestFor: Literature reviews, study/experiment design, and rigorous written research.

writing-content.name: Writing / Content
writing-content.description: A writing room — research, drafting, editing, and fact-checking against sources.
writing-content.bestFor: Articles, documentation, and content where the claims must hold up.

security-audit.name: Security / Audit
security-audit.description: Security-first — threat modeling and authorized-testing advice, with the security gate leading the review.
security-audit.bestFor: Security reviews and audits of an existing codebase.
security-audit.elevateNote: The `security` gate is the primary reviewer here, backed by the threat-modeler and pentester-advisor. The pentester-advisor never advises on active testing without the owner's explicit, documented authorization.

solo-minimal.name: Solo Minimal
solo-minimal.description: The lightest team — one builder, testing, docs, plus the core four gates and memory. Lowest token cost.
solo-minimal.bestFor: A solo developer who wants review + persistent memory without the full org.

boss-1.title: Primary Orchestrator
boss-1.summary: The single agent you talk to; decomposes goals, delegates, integrates, and brings one reconciled recommendation.

boss-2.title: Independent Auditor
boss-2.summary: A second, independent take on expensive-to-be-wrong decisions before they reach you.

critics.title: Correctness & Trust Gate
critics.summary: Read-only. PASS/BLOCK against the Charter's non-negotiables and past lessons.

security.title: Exploitability Gate
security.summary: Read-only. Audits code for secrets, injection, auth/authz holes, dependency vulns, and whether the project's trust promises hold.

research-head.title: Head of Research
research-head.summary: Routes research questions, makes researchers coordinate, and synthesizes one decision-ready briefing.

dev-head.title: Head of Development
dev-head.summary: Turns build goals into shipped, tested, reviewed work; runs the build/test/debug loop and routes through both gates.

tech-researcher.title: Technical Researcher
tech-researcher.summary: 'How do we build X well?' — best practices, the right library/pattern, real tradeoffs, always cited.

domain-researcher.title: Domain & Market Researcher
domain-researcher.summary: The real need, what already exists, the gaps — scope-shaping, and gated by critics against the Charter.

developer-1.title: Senior Production Developer
developer-1.summary: Ships durable code — load, failure, edge cases, security — never a happy-path demo.

developer-2.title: Senior Production Developer (peer)
developer-2.summary: Peer to developer-1; its own git worktree and surface when building in parallel.

testing.title: Testing
testing.summary: Writes tests that try to break the work; holds the project's test bar; proves the Charter's invariants.

debugger.title: Debugger & Durability Skeptic
debugger.summary: Finds real root causes and flags happy-path shortcuts so builders write durable code by habit.

design.title: UI/UX Design
design.summary: Bound first by the project's own design guidance in the Charter, and only secondarily by market trends.

technical-writer.title: Technical Writer
technical-writer.summary: Clear, honest documentation verified against the code; never documents a capability the code lacks.

data-engineer.title: Data Engineer
data-engineer.summary: Robust pipelines/ETL and data quality that survive dirty data, schema drift, and scale.

ml-engineer.title: ML Engineer
ml-engineer.summary: Honestly-evaluated, reproducible models — no leakage, real baselines, documented failure modes.

literature-reviewer.title: Literature Reviewer
literature-reviewer.summary: Surveys prior work and tracks citations honestly; every claim traceable to a real source.

methodologist.title: Methodologist
methodologist.summary: Experiment design and statistical rigor; guards against p-hacking, leakage, and unsupported claims.

editor.title: Editor
editor.summary: Structure, clarity, and voice — without changing meaning or introducing new claims.

fact-checker.title: Fact-Checker
fact-checker.summary: The correctness gate for prose — verifies every claim against a real source; blocks the unsupported.

threat-modeler.title: Threat Modeler
threat-modeler.summary: Maps attack surface and threats to concrete mitigations. Advisory — never runs live attacks.

pentester-advisor.title: Penetration-Testing Advisor
pentester-advisor.summary: Advises on test methodology within authorized, documented scope; the human executes, never the advisor.

marketing.title: Real-User Signal & Growth
marketing.summary: Optional add-on. Surfaces real user pain from public communities; reads signal, never spams or impersonates.

headsAreOptional: Some lighter packs do not staff a research-head or dev-head. When a pack omits a head, boss-1 manages those specialists directly, and any role with a reportsToFallback reports to that fallback (boss-1). threat-modeler and pentester-advisor report up the security line to boss-1/security and need no head.
